repository.name: sendlog
repository.description: Climbing app

commit.message:
Add session type selector, speed climb sessions, lightbox swipe

Session setup:
- New type selection screen before starting: choose Bouldering, Rope Climbing, Speed Climbing (multi-select, at least one required)
- Selected types determine which buttons appear in the active session screen
- Unselected types accessible via a 'See More' toggle button

Session active screen:
- Buttons filtered to selected types + Log Project always present
- 'See More' reveals the other type buttons without cluttering the main view
- Speed Climb Session button adds a full speed session object inline (not a form)

Speed Climb Session card (live in session):
- Separate rest timer shows time elapsed since last attempt (ticks every second)
- 'Add Attempt' button opens an inline form: enter time in seconds, or tap 'Fell' for a fall
- Each attempt shows ✓ time or ✗ Fell with rest gap displayed between entries
- Best time for the session highlighted with a PB badge
- Speed sessions excluded from regular climb stats (sends, grade breakdown, etc.)
- Speed attempt totals shown in the End Session confirmation

Lightbox:
- Touch swipe left/right to navigate between photos (50px threshold)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 07-02 perf regression + add segment-level cohort analysis (09-25/26)

07-01: Limit Other Banks to big_nationals/digital_banks/fed_district
with only PAYMENT/TRANSFER suffixes (was generating 500+ patterns from
all competitor categories × 5 suffixes, choking str.contains regex).

07-02: Batch regex matching in groups of 80 to prevent massive
alternation patterns from hanging.

09-25: New segment cohort data cell -- classifies each mailed account
into challenge segment (NU, TH-10, TH-15, TH-20, TH-25) and computes
monthly spend from -6 to +36 months relative to mail date. Compares
responders vs non-responders within each segment.

09-26: Multi-panel spend trajectory chart + DID lift bar chart by
segment and time window (3mo, 6mo, 12mo, 24mo, 36mo post).

Deepen epistemology: Cox's theorem, severity, structural realism, eliminative inference

- Ch1: Name inference to best explanation, add Cox's theorem as logical foundation
- Ch3: Add theory-ladenness of observation, no-miracles argument, chi-squared math
- Ch4: Name underdetermination, explain concordance as its antidote
- Ch5: Compress era descriptions, add Duhem-Quine problem
- Ch7: Reframe Hubble tension as productive anomaly
- Ch8: Split convergence into concordance/consilience/robustness (Whewell, Wimsatt),
  add severe testing (Mayo), structural realism (Worrall), eliminative inference (Norton),
  Bayesian Occam factor for simplicity
- Add KaTeX for math rendering, 6 math-peek components throughout
- Second slop pass: fix remaining binary contrasts, triples, em-dashes, throat-clearing

commit.message:
Fix image translation showing same language (ja→ja) by preventing sourceLang override

The initial paragraph setup useEffect was overriding the correctly detected
sourceLang from image data with default 'en' (no param passed from image modal).
This caused language monitoring to flip targetLang to 'ja', triggering
retranslation with en→ja on Japanese source text.

- Skip sourceLang override when fromImageTranslation (already set by image data useEffect)
- Use actual detected language codes in translationData instead of defaults
- Sync prevTargetLangRef to prevent false retranslation trigger

fix: auto-wrap evaluate expressions and remove CORS-breaking stealth headers

- Add getEvaluationFallback() to automatically wrap top-level return/await
  expressions in IIFE wrappers, fixing ~22 out of 28 run_javascript 500
  errors seen in navibench task 80
- Replace Sec-Fetch-*/Upgrade-Insecure-Requests stealth headers with
  locale-only context options to avoid breaking SPA CORS preflights
  (fixes Resy venue page hydration failure)
- Deprecate getStealthHeaders() in favor of getStealthContextOptions()

errors counted. Guard the issue with && num('HTML_ERRORS') > 0 to
avoid opening non-actionable tickets.

fix: detect claude-in-chrome at agent runtime instead of config file

claude-in-chrome is injected by a Chrome extension at runtime and never
appears in ~/.claude.json mcpServers. The preflight script could never
detect it, always reporting Grok as "not ready" for chrome users.

- SKILL.md: agent checks for mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools first,
  skips shell preflight entirely if present (self-evident to agent)
- grok_setup.sh: add --backend override flag as escape hatch for
  callers that know their backend (e.g. other scripts/integrations)

fix(supplier-debt): use tposFetch helper with 401 retry for all API calls

Replaced 13 raw getAuthHeader+fetch calls with tposFetch() helper that
delegates to tokenManager.authenticatedFetch() - automatically retries
on 401 with fresh token (refresh_token → password → SwitchCompany).

fix: sidebar visual parity — full-size cameo icons, two-tone power bar, clean credits

Three fixes for sidebar rendering:
1. Cameo icons now use actual image dimensions (32x24 LORES) scaled to
   fill full 64x48 slots, fixing manifest/PNG dimension mismatch that
   caused icons to render at quarter size
2. Power bar shows two-tone gauge: green (produced) from bottom, red
   (over-consumed) above, with white divider line
3. Credits area uses semi-transparent background blending with sidebar
   texture, silo capacity displayed inline, button row backgrounds
   also semi-transparent

commit.message:
fix: SubSquare API + delegator snapshot timeout (#116)

- SubSquare deprecated /gov2/referendums (404). Updated Polkadot adapter
  to use /summary endpoint only, which still provides referenda counts
  and active track data.
- Delegator snapshot was timing out (>100s, Cloudflare 524). Refactored
  into chunked Inngest steps (100 DReps each, <60s per step). Added
  coverage-based skip logic (95% threshold) replacing the broken
  existingCount > 100 check that allowed partial writes to block retries.
- Treasury sync: 14/14 failures were deployment restarts, not code bugs.
  No fix needed.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove bun export condition (#29)

* fix: remove "bun" export condition to fix dual-module-instance bug

When repterm is installed as an npm dependency, the "bun" export condition
causes CLI (from dist/) and user test code (from src/) to use different
TestRegistry instances, resulting in "beforeAll must be called inside a
describe() block" errors. Removing the "bun" condition ensures both resolve
to the same dist/ module. This follows the Playwright pattern and is the
standard approach for CLI tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update bun.lock

* fix: add build step before tests in CI

Without the "bun" export condition, workspace packages resolve to dist/
instead of src/. The test job needs to build packages first so dist/
exists when tests import from workspace dependencies like repterm-api.
